channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Tejas Mk2 Enters Critical Phase as Taxi Trials Near and First Flight Clearance Process Advances
item.description: India’s Tejas Mk2 Medium Weight Fighter (MWF) program is now entering a final phase, with ground trials and certification activities accelerating ahead of its maiden flight. According to the latest developments, low-speed and high-speed taxi trials are expected to commence shortly, marking a key milestone in validating the aircraft’s ground handling characteristics, braking performance, and directional stability before it transitions into flight testing.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: AMCA Program Gets Boost as MIDHANI Starts Delivering Titanium Alloys and Advanced Materials
item.description: India’s push for indigenous aerospace manufacturing has taken another significant step forward as Mishra Dhatu Nigam Limited has successfully developed and started supplying titanium alloy slabs for the airframe of the Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft. The development strengthens India’s domestic materials ecosystem for next-generation combat aircraft and supports the broader effort to reduce dependence on imported aerospace-grade metals.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: From IAF to Navy: Ghatak UCAV Could Get Aircraft Carrier Version for Indian Ocean Deployment
item.description: India’s ambitious stealth unmanned combat aerial vehicle program, the Ghatak UCAV, has entered a new phase as it transitions from a long-running research project into a potential operational platform for the Indian armed forces. Originally conceived as a technology demonstrator under the Autonomous Unmanned Research Aircraft (AURA) program, the system is now evolving into a high-priority acquisition program after the Indian Air Force expressed interest in inducting a sizable fleet of the stealth drones.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Stand-Off Precision Warfare – The Evolution of India’s Non-Contact Strike Doctrine in Operation Sindoor 2
item.description: In the event of renewed hostilities with Pakistan, Operation Sindoor 2 would represent a refined and expanded iteration of India’s non-contact warfare doctrine, honed through the lessons of the 2025 four-day operation. Rather than risking large-scale ground incursions or crossing the Line of Control (LoC) en masse, Indian forces would rely on calibrated, stand-off precision strikes to dismantle Pakistan’s terror infrastructure while keeping escalation firmly below the nuclear threshold. Post-Sindoor reforms, including accelerated indigenous missile production and drone integration, would enable sustained operations over weeks rather than days, imposing disproportionate costs on the adversary’s proxy networks.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Develops Indigenous Diagnostic System for MiG-29K Amid Major Fleet Upgrade
item.description: The Indian Navy has taken a significant step toward improving the operational readiness of its MiG-29K carrier-based fighter fleet by opening the NASK 1-29K Ground Automated Test System to private industry through a Request for Information (RFI) issued on April 11, 2026. The move is part of the broader Project 45A, which aims to indigenize critical support infrastructure and address longstanding serviceability challenges associated with the fleet.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: D³ Startup Unveils Plans for Indigenous Loitering Munition with 50+ km Range
item.description: Patna-based deep-tech startup D³ (D Cube) has announced ambitious plans to develop a next-generation indigenous loitering munition system, marking another step in India’s rapidly evolving unmanned warfare ecosystem. Focused on advanced engineering across robotics and aerospace domains, the startup is positioning itself within a critical segment of modern battlefield technology where precision, autonomy, and cost-effectiveness converge.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Saab Offers Lightweight Torpedo to Indian Navy for Advanced Anti-Submarine Warfare in Littoral Waters
item.description: Saab has pitched its Lightweight Torpedo (SLWT) to the Indian Navy as a next-generation anti-submarine warfare (ASW) solution, tailored for the complex and cluttered acoustic environments of littoral waters. As India sharpens its focus on undersea dominance in the Indian Ocean Region, particularly in shallow coastal zones where submarine detection is inherently challenging, the SLWT is being positioned as a flexible, multi-platform weapon system capable of delivering high kill probability against modern submarine threats.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: NSA Ajit Doval Holds High-Level Talks In Riyadh; Focus On Bilateral Ties, Regional Security
item.description: India’s National Security Advisor Ajit Doval paid an official visit to Riyadh on Sunday, engaging in a series of high-level meetings with senior Saudi leadership to strengthen bilateral relations, enhance energy cooperation, and discuss key regional developments.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India, US Set For Crucial Trade Talks In Washington From Apr 20
item.description: A team of around a dozen Indian officials is set to arrive in Washington, D.C. on April 20 for a three-day round of negotiations with US authorities, marking the next step in discussions on the first phase of a proposed bilateral trade agreement (BTA).
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: US Navy seizes Iranian-flagged ship near Hormuz, Tehran vows swift response
item.description: The United States attacked and seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship it said had tried to evade a naval blockade near the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday, and Iran’s joint military command vowed to respond, throwing a fragile ceasefire into question days before it expires.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pak’s Mediation May Led To US’s Humiliation Rather Than Victory In West Asia Conflict: Report
item.description: “By relying on Pakistan to mediate the most acute national security concerns, US President Donald Trump essentially does the equivalent of hiring a child molester to teach in a kindergarten,” Michael Rubin, a senior fellow at the American Enterprise Institute, wrote in The Sunday Guardian about Islamabad’s mediation in the West Asia conflict.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Air India expects to complete retrofit of 7 more Boeing 787-8 planes
item.description: Air India expects to complete retrofitting of seven more legacy Boeing 787-8 planes this year as it upgrades the fleet of these aircraft that is key to the airline’s long-haul operations. The loss-making carrier, which is in the midst of an ambitious transformation plan, has received the first of its retrofitted legacy Boeing 787-8 plane, which has 250 seats in three class configuration of business, premium economy and economy. The plane landed in the national capital on April 13.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: South Korean President’s India Visit To Mark New Phase In Bilateral Ties
item.description: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung’s three-day visit to India starting Sunday is likely to serve as a significant milestone in enhancing India-South Korea bilateral relationship, extending beyond the economic sphere to include security, culture, and people-to-people connections, a report said on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Tamil Leaders Thank India For Supporting Sri Lanka, Seek OCI Status For Indian-Origin People
item.description: Ceylon Workers Congress President Senthil Thondaman said on Sunday that it was an honour to meet Vice President C.P. Radhakrishnan and expressed gratitude to Indian government for their continuous support to Sri Lanka during the Cyclone Ditwah.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Sends Humanitarian Assistance To African Nations As US-Iran War Exacerbates Food Crisis: Report
item.description: In recent weeks, India has sent 1,000 metric tonnes of rice to drought-stricken Malawi, 1,000 metric tonnes to Burkina Faso, and 500 metric tonnes plus relief supplies, including tents, hygiene kits, and medicines to Mozambique, but will its sole efforts solve the issue, an article in a South African news outlet asked.
item.dc:creator: admin
